fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, and the West Indies won the inaugural tournament by defeating Australia in the final.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Badminton became an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Games, with Indonesia and South Korea dominating the early medal tables.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil is the most successful nation in FIFA World Cup history, having won the tournament five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
The first Wimbledon Championship was held in 1877, and Spencer Gore won the inaugural gentlemen's singles title.
The Grand Slam in tennis consists of four major tournaments: the Australian Open, the French Open, Wimbledon, and the US Open, all played on different court surfaces.
Basketball was invented in 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a physical education instructor in Springfield, Massachusetts, who used peach baskets as the original goals.
The NBA was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA), and adopted the name National Basketball Association in 1949 after merging with the rival National Basketball League.
Field hockey has been part of the Olympic Games since 1908 for men and 1980 for women, with India winning eight men's Olympic gold medals, more than any other nation.
The Stanley Cup, first awarded in 1893, is the oldest trophy competed for by professional athletes in North America and is the championship prize of the NHL.
The first Formula 1 World Championship race was held in 1950 at Silverstone Circuit in the United Kingdom, and Giuseppe Farina won the inaugural race for Alfa Romeo.
Michael Schumacher and Lewis Hamilton share the record for most Formula 1 World Drivers' Championships, with seven titles each.
Usain Bolt of Jamaica set the current world record in the 100m sprint, running 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin.
The modern Olympic Games were revived in 1896 in Athens, Greece, largely due to the efforts of French educator Pierre de Coubertin.
